Name: simpsons-paradox-loop-01
Description: Loop 1: kidney-stone synthetic example (two stone sizes, two treatments).

Datasets.Description: Table: Datasets — source datasets for the kidney-stone Simpson's Paradox demonstration.

schema.name: DatasetId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for the dataset (slug).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display name, mirrors DatasetId.

schema.name: Source
schema.Description: Human-readable description of where the dataset came from.

schema.name: SourceUrl
schema.Description: URL pointing to the original data source.

schema.name: IngestDate
schema.Description: Date the dataset was ingested.

Observations.Description: Table: Observations — one row per (dataset, stone size, treatment) combination recording successes and trials.

schema.name: ObservationId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this observation record (slug).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label for this observation.

schema.name: Dataset
schema.Description: FK to the parent Dataset.

schema.name: StoneSize
schema.Description: Stone size category: small or large.

schema.name: Treatment
schema.Description: Treatment applied: A or B.

schema.name: Successes
schema.Description: Number of successful outcomes in this cell.

schema.name: Trials
schema.Description: Total number of trials in this cell.

PooledRates.Description: Table: PooledRates — pooled (collapsed across strata) success counts and rate per dataset+treatment pair.

schema.name: PooledRateId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this pooled-rate record (slug, e.g. kidney-A).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label for this pooled rate.

schema.name: Dataset
schema.Description: FK to the parent Dataset.

schema.name: Treatment
schema.Description: Treatment identifier: A or B.

schema.name: PooledSuccesses
schema.Description: Sum of successes across all strata for this dataset+treatment.

schema.name: PooledTrials
schema.Description: Sum of trials across all strata for this dataset+treatment.

schema.name: PooledRate
schema.Description: Pooled success rate: PooledSuccesses / PooledTrials.

StratumRates.Description: Table: StratumRates — per-stratum success counts and rate per dataset+stone-size+treatment combination.

schema.name: StratumRateId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this stratum-rate record (slug, e.g. kidney-small-A).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label for this stratum rate.

schema.name: Dataset
schema.Description: FK to the parent Dataset.

schema.name: StoneSize
schema.Description: Stone size stratum: small or large.

schema.name: Treatment
schema.Description: Treatment identifier: A or B.

schema.name: StratumSuccesses
schema.Description: Sum of successes for this dataset+stone-size+treatment cell.

schema.name: StratumTrials
schema.Description: Sum of trials for this dataset+stone-size+treatment cell.

schema.name: StratumRate
schema.Description: Per-stratum success rate: StratumSuccesses / StratumTrials.

ReversalDetections.Description: Table: ReversalDetections — one row per dataset identifying whether a Simpson's Paradox reversal occurred.

schema.name: ReversalDetectionId
schema.Description: Unique identifier for this reversal detection record (slug).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label for this detection.

schema.name: Dataset
schema.Description: FK to the dataset being tested for reversal.

schema.name: GroupA
schema.Description: Label for the first treatment group being compared (e.g. A).

schema.name: GroupB
schema.Description: Label for the second treatment group being compared (e.g. B).

schema.name: PooledWinner
schema.Description: Treatment with the higher pooled rate — the misleading pooled winner.

schema.name: PerStratumSummary
schema.Description: Free-text human summary of per-stratum comparison direction.

schema.name: ReversalFlag
schema.Description: True when the pooled winner differs from the per-stratum winner in all strata — confirming Simpson's Paradox.

schema.name: DecidingStratum
schema.Description: The stratum whose confounding effect drives the paradox (e.g. large stones).

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Loop-01 Simpson's Paradox rulebook: kidney-stone two-treatment synthetic example. Tables: Datasets, Observations, PooledRates, StratumRates, ReversalDetections.
